metadata.description: dev-factory — a self-hosting 'dark factory' for software engineering. A kernel that advances a typed knowledge lattice by running a bounded, autonomous outer loop over a coordination corpus of work tickets, with earned-and-mechanically-revocable autonomy. The kernel + kits are plugins; the runtime (dev-server) ships separately via pip/uvx. Built on harness-forge's lattice kernel (vendored, drift-gated), with a 9-dimension-faithful design proven end to end across Crawl → Walk → Run → Fly.

plugins.name: dev-kernel
plugins.description: The invariant kernel: the typed contracts (cell · ticket · ledger · activity · dispatch-policy · kit · adapter · naming schemas), the two state machines (the vendored 8-state cell maturity machine + the ticket lifecycle), the four protective gates + two lifecycle predicates that mechanize the immutable/rewritable boundary, the compass (deterministic selection), the validation path (signal minted from a verifier's exit status), the autonomy trust-trajectory with mechanical demotion, distillation, the kit-conform boundary gate, and the 12-agent roster across 8 compound skills. The central reconciliation: ticket `done` ⟺ the target cell advances through the same gate-signal — the board cannot disagree with the lattice.

plugins.name: dev-kit-corpus
plugins.description: The corpus family kit — binds the dev-kernel contracts to building and validating a knowledge corpus (spec · rubric · pattern · methodology cells), with its own ontology, rubric manifest, validation harness (doc-check · rubric-check), dispatch policy, and seed patterns. Stateless: it binds the kernel, it never forks it (check-kit-conform enforces zero kernel edits).

plugins.name: dev-kit-app
plugins.description: The application family kit — binds the dev-kernel contracts to building and validating application code (capability · protocol cells validated by a passing test suite). Exists primarily to prove the kernel/kit boundary's falsification test: adding a second family required ZERO kernel edits.
